description: Align register and sync commands with Go CLI behavior: config-driven network/namespace fallback for register, sync --clean help text parity, and integration test coverage for both commands in Go-style project configurations.

userStories.title: Register Config-Driven Network Fallback
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `treb register --tx-hash 0x...` to resolve the network from treb.toml/config.local.json when --network and --rpc-url are omitted, so that register works without explicit flags in configured projects like the Go CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: `treb register --tx-hash 0x... --network anvil-31337` still works (explicit flag path unchanged)
`treb register --tx-hash 0x... --rpc-url http://...` still works (explicit URL path unchanged)
`treb register --tx-hash 0x...` (no network flag) resolves network from treb.toml config when available
`treb register --tx-hash 0x...` errors with 'no active network set in config, --network flag is required' when config has no network and no flag is provided
`resolve_rpc_endpoints` is called with dotenv already loaded (via resolve_config or resolve_rpc_endpoints which calls load_dotenv internally)
Unit test for fallback logic in register.rs
Typecheck passes: `cargo check -p treb-cli`

userStories.title: Register Config-Driven Namespace Fallback
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `treb register` to derive the namespace from resolve_config() when --namespace is omitted, so that register respects treb.toml namespace settings like the Go CLI does.
acceptanceCriteria: `treb register --namespace production ...` still overrides config namespace
`treb register --tx-hash 0x... --network anvil-31337` (no --namespace) uses namespace from config resolution, or 'default' if no config exists
Generated deployment IDs use the config-derived namespace when --namespace is not provided
Unit test for namespace resolution priority: explicit flag > config > 'default'
Typecheck passes: `cargo check -p treb-cli`

userStories.title: Sync Clean Flag Description and Help Alignment
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `sync --clean` help text to match the Go CLI description so that the Rust CLI help output is consistent with Go.
acceptanceCriteria: `treb sync --help` shows --clean with description 'Remove invalid entries while syncing'
build.rs and main.rs descriptions for sync --clean match
Golden help snapshots updated for sync help changes
Typecheck passes: `cargo check -p treb-cli`

userStories.title: Integration Tests for Config-Driven Register and Sync
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests verifying register and sync work correctly in Go-style project configurations where network/namespace come from config rather than explicit flags.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one test proves register resolves network from config (Anvil-backed, similar to existing register_basic pattern)
At least one test proves register errors correctly when config has no network and no flag
At least one test proves namespace comes from config when --namespace is omitted
Sync clean flag acceptance and help text test passes
All existing register and sync tests continue to pass
`cargo test -p treb-cli --test integration_register` passes
`cargo test -p treb-cli --test integration_sync` passes
